classification.cautionReason: This work discusses heresy in order to refute it; do not classify it as heretical merely from its title.

source.title: NPNF1-04. Augustine: The Writings Against the Manichaeans and Against the Donatists

verses.book: On the Morals of the Catholic Church.
verses.text: Let us see how the Lord Himself in the gospel has taught us to live; how, too, Paul the apostle,—for the Manichæans dare not reject these Scriptures.

verses.book: On the Morals of the Catholic Church.
verses.text: Let us hear, O Christ, what chief end Thou dost prescribe to us; and that is evidently the chief end after which we are told to strive with supreme affection.

verses.book: On the Morals of the Catholic Church.
verses.text: "Thou shalt love," He says, "the Lord thy God."

verses.book: On the Morals of the Catholic Church.
verses.text: Tell me also, I pray Thee, what must be the measure of love; for I fear lest the desire enkindled in my heart should either exceed or come short in fervor.

verses.book: On the Morals of the Catholic Church.
On the Morals of the Catholic Church.
On the Morals of the Catholic Church.
On the Morals of the Catholic Church.
On the Morals of the Catholic Church.
On the Morals of the Catholic Church.
On the Morals of the Catholic Church.
verses.text: I might, perhaps, wish more if I could see the possibility of more.

verses.book: On the Morals of the Catholic Church.
verses.text: What does Paul say on this?

verses.book: On the Morals of the Catholic Church.
verses.text: "We know," he says, "that all things issue in good to them that love God."

verses.book: On the Morals of the Catholic Church.
verses.text: Let him, too, say what is the measure of love.

verses.book: On the Morals of the Catholic Church.
verses.text: "Who then," he says, "shall separate us from the love of Christ? shall tribulation, or distress, or persecution, or famine, or nakedness, or peril, or the sword?"

verses.book: On the Morals of the Catholic Church.
verses.text: We have heard, then, what and how much we must love; this we must strive after, and to this we must refer all our plans.

verses.book: On the Morals of the Catholic Church.
verses.text: The perfection of all our good things and our perfect good is God.

verses.book: On the Morals of the Catholic Church.
verses.text: We must neither come short of this nor go beyond it: the one is dangerous, the other impossible.
